content.subtitle: Some verbs followed by -ing
items: admit, avoid, deny, discuss, dislike, enjoy, feel like, finish, give up, mention, mind, practise, suggest, take up

content.subtitle: Useful notes
content.description: When a verb follows a preposition, it takes the -ing form.

notes.note: Some phrases end with the preposition *to*. These phrases are also followed by the -ing form, not an infinitive.
The -ing form can also function as the subject of a sentence.

content.subtitle: Some verbs followed by full infinitive (to + verb)
items: advise, afford, agree, choose, decide, expect, help, hope, invite, learn, manage, offer, plan, pretend, promise, refuse, seem, teach, tell, want, would like

content.subtitle: Be careful
items: Some verbs (let, make) are typically followed by an object + bare infinitive (without to).
When make is used in the passive, it is followed by a full infinitive: The students were made to rewrite their essays.
Some verbs can take either the infinitive alone or object + full infinitive: She needs him to explain the problem.

content.subtitle: Same or nearly same meaning
items: Verbs like begin, continue, hate, like, love, prefer and start can be followed by either form with little difference in meaning.

content.subtitle: Meaning changes
rows: recall something that happened in the past
not forget to carry out a planned task
be unable to recall a past event or action
fail to do something that was planned
cease doing something permanently or for a time
pause in order to do something else
attempt something as an experiment or solution
make an effort or attempt to do something

sections.title: F: Original key word transformation practice
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it keeps the same meaning as the first.

items.sentence:
Mia began the class project before the lesson on -ing & infinitive. **REMEMBER**
The class project ...... before the lesson on -ing & infinitive.

Leo began the study group before the lesson on -ing & infinitive. **REMEMBER**
The study group ...... before the lesson on -ing & infinitive.

Sara began the exam workshop before the lesson on -ing & infinitive. **STOPPED**
The exam workshop ...... before the lesson on -ing & infinitive.

Tom began the online lesson before the lesson on -ing & infinitive. **TRY**
The online lesson ...... before the lesson on -ing & infinitive.

Nina began the school club before the lesson on -ing & infinitive. **MADE**
The school club ...... before the lesson on -ing & infinitive.

Oscar began the revision session before the lesson on -ing & infinitive. **MADE**
The revision session ...... before the lesson on -ing & infinitive.
